message:
fix(node): eliminate expect/unwrap panics in favor of proper error
propagation across cancellation, marker stream, compatibility gate,
economic integration, and threshold signature modules

Systematic removal of panic-on-impossible patterns in favor of fail-safe
error propagation:

Control plane:
- cancellation_protocol.rs: add InvariantViolation error variant with
  ERR_CANCEL_INVARIANT code; replace .expect("records non-empty: just
  pushed") with .ok_or_else(CancelProtocolError::InvariantViolation)
  so a hypothetical empty-after-push scenario returns an error instead
  of panicking the process
- marker_stream.rs: replace .expect("markers non-empty after push")
  with .ok_or_else(MarkerStreamError::IntegrityFailure) for the same
  defensive pattern

Policy:
- compatibility_gate.rs: extract digest_prefix_u64() helper that uses
  .get(..8) with fallback zero-fill instead of .try_into().expect() on
  SHA-256 digest slicing — avoids panic if digest is ever truncated

Security:
- economic_integration.rs: replace .expect("guarded: early return on
  is_empty() above") with let-else pattern that returns empty Vec,
  removing the coupling between the guard check and the .last() call
- threshold_sig.rs: extract digest_prefix_u64() helper (same pattern as
  compatibility_gate) to eliminate .try_into().expect() in both
  verify_signature() and sign() functions

Meta:
- .beads/issues.jsonl: sync issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ui92.4","title":"bd-ui92 support: remove expect panic in zk_attestation compliance counters","description":"Replace map get_mut(...).expect(...) counter updates in security/zk_attestation compliance report generation with explicit fail-safe update logic; validate via rch offloaded check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyGrove","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:19:54.720882542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T05:45:04.806905661Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T05:45:04.806881446Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ui92.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ui92","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:19:54.720882542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":83,"issue_id":"bd-ui92.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented panic-path hardening in crates/franken-node/src/security/zk_attestation.rs:887-920 by replacing get_mut(...).expect(...) counter increments with an explicit fail-safe helper (increment_counter) that inserts missing keys instead of panicking.\n\nValidation evidence (offloaded via rch):\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/target_rch_rainygrove_bd_ui924_check7 cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib --message-format short -> exit 0 (Finished dev profile in 2m33s).\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/target_rch_rainygrove_bd_ui924_clippy1 cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --lib -- -D warnings -> exit 0 (Finished dev profile in 2m36s).\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/security/zk_attestation.rs -> exit 0.\n\nInfra note: one earlier rch check attempt with /tmp target failed due remote os error 28 (no space left on device); resolved by switching CARGO_TARGET_DIR to /data/projects/franken_node/...","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:45:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ui92.5","title":"bd-ui92 support: remove expect panic in time_travel capture_frame","description":"Replace CaptureSession::capture_frame frames.last().expect(...) panic path in runtime/time_travel.rs with explicit fail-safe error propagation; validate via rch offloaded check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyGrove","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:46:24.504282337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T05:57:26.252081057Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T05:57:26.252052454Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ui92.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ui92","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:46:24.504282337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":86,"issue_id":"bd-ui92.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented panic-path hardening in crates/franken-node/src/runtime/time_travel.rs:447-468.

Change:
- In CaptureSession::capture_frame, replaced `self.frames.last().expect("frames non-empty after push")` with explicit `ok_or_else(...)` returning `TimeTravelError::SnapshotCorrupt { detail: "capture invariant violated: frame missing after append" }`.
- Preserves invariant enforcement while removing production panic path and propagating structured error.

Validation evidence:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/target_rch_rainygrove_bd_ui925_check cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib --message-format short` -> exit 0 (Finished dev profile in 2m18s).
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/target_rch_rainygrove_bd_ui925_clippy2 cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --lib -- -D warnings` -> exit 0 (Finished dev profile in 2m29s).
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/runtime/time_travel.rs` -> exit 0.

Infra note:
- One intermediate clippy attempt (`..._clippy`) became rch progress-stale and was cancelled; rerun (`..._clippy2`) passed cleanly.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:57:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ui92.6","title":"bd-ui92 support: remove expect panics in hardware_planner register paths","description":"Replace register_profile/register_policy post-insert get(...).expect(...) in runtime/hardware_planner.rs with entry-based insertion returning references without panic paths; validate via rch offloaded check/clippy.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyGrove","created_at":"2026-03-04T06:00:13.673187023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T06:21:09.102480497Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ui92.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ui92","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T06:00:13.673187023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":89,"issue_id":"bd-ui92.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented panic-path hardening in `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/hardware_planner.rs`:

- `register_profile`: replaced `contains_key + insert + get(...).expect(...)` with `BTreeMap::entry` match.
  - `Entry::Occupied(_)` -> `Err(HardwarePlannerError::DuplicateProfile { ... })`
  - `Entry::Vacant(entry)` -> emit audit event then `Ok(entry.insert(profile))`
- `register_policy`: same `entry`-based conversion.
- Added `btree_map::Entry` import.

This removes two production `expect(...)` panic paths while preserving duplicate detection and audit emission semantics.

Validation status (offloaded via `rch`):
- Formatting: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/runtime/hardware_planner.rs` -> exit 0.
- Multiple offloaded `cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib --message-format short` attempts (different target dirs and incremental settings) repeatedly reached `Checking frankenengine-node` but hit `rch` execution instability:
  - timeout envelope exits: `exit 137` (~301s)
  - progress-stale detector windows requiring cancellation
- Because of this infra behavior, I do not yet have a clean `cargo check`/`cargo clippy` pass for this shard despite code changes being applied.

Current state: code-complete, validation blocked by remote execution instability.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T06:21:09Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ukh7","title":"[10.19] Implement privacy envelope layer: secure aggregation + differential-privacy budget enforcement.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.19 — Adversarial Trust Commons Execution Track (9M)

Why This Exists:
ATC federated intelligence track for privacy-preserving cross-deployment threat learning, robust aggregation, and verifier-backed trust metrics.

Task Objective:
Implement privacy envelope layer: secure aggregation + differential-privacy budget enforcement.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Participant contributions are aggregated without exposing per-participant raw values; privacy budget accounting is deterministic and policy-gated.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/atc_privacy_envelope.md`, `src/federation/atc_secure_aggregation.rs`, `artifacts/10.19/atc_privacy_budget_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-ukh7/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-ukh7/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.19] Implement privacy envelope layer: secure aggregation + differential-privacy budget enforcement.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.19] Implement privacy envelope layer: secure aggregation + differential-privacy budget enforcement." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.19] Implement privacy envelope layer: secure aggregation + differential-privacy budget enforcement." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.19] Implement privacy envelope layer: secure aggregation + differential-privacy budget enforcement." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.19] Implement privacy envelope layer: secure aggregation + differential-privacy budget enforcement." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Participant contributions are aggregated without exposing per-participant raw values; privacy budget accounting is deterministic and policy-gated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:05.585135739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:07:28.283739384Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:07:28.283713085Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-19","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukh7","depends_on_id":"bd-3aqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:14:52.356888873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-unkm","title":"[16] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 16

Task Objective:
Create a hard section completion gate that verifies all implemented behavior in this section with comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e scripts, and detailed structured logging evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Section test matrix covers happy-path, edge-case, and adversarial/error-path scenarios for all section deliverables.
- E2E scripts execute representative end-user workflows and policy/control-plane transitions for this section.
- Structured logs include stable event/error codes, trace correlation IDs, and enough context for deterministic replay triage.
- Verification report is deterministic and machine-readable for CI/release gating.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-level test matrix and coverage summary artifact.
- E2E script suite with pass/fail outputs and reproducible fixtures/seeds.
- Structured log validation report and traceability bundle.
- Gate verdict artifact consumable by release automation.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_16/bd-unkm/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_16/bd-unkm/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests must validate contracts, invariants, and failure semantics.
- E2E tests must validate cross-component behavior from user/operator entrypoints to persisted effects.
- Logging must support root-cause analysis without hidden context requirements.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[16] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[16] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[16] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[16] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[16] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Section 16 verification gate runs all scientific-contribution check scripts and confirms 100% pass rate.
2. Gate validates: (a) open specs are published and versioned, (b) datasets are published with DOIs, (c) methodology documents are peer-reviewed, (d) external evaluations are completed, (e) transparent reports are published, (f) output contracts (reports, replications, tools) are met.
3. Publication completeness: >= 3 reproducible reports, >= 2 external replications, >= 2 red-team engagements, >= 1 dataset with DOI.
4. Gate produces section_16_verification_summary.md with per-contribution status and publication checklist.
5. Any contribution below target is flagged with gap analysis and remediation timeline.
6. The gate itself has a unit test verifying correct aggregation of sub-check results.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildMountain","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.265005180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:04:35.347545559Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:04:35.347505875Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-16","test-obligations","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-10ee","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.511640251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-1dpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:24:23.472704262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-1sgr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.462746243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.660003045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-2twu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:20:48.805444370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-33u2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.363597834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-3id1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.560227107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-e5cz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.413078314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-f955","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.708143710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-unkm","depends_on_id":"bd-nbh7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:31.611242326Z  {"id":"bd-uo4","title":"[10.0] Deliver dual-layer lockstep oracle program (L1 product + L2 engine boundary + release-policy linkage).","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.0 — Top 10 Initiative Tracking (Initiative #4)
Cross-references: 9A.4, 9B.4, 9C.4, 9D.4

Why This Exists:
The dual-layer lockstep oracle is the #4 strategic initiative. It provides externally verifiable compatibility and runtime-integrity guarantees by running Node, Bun, and franken_node in synchronized scenarios at two layers: L1 (product-visible behavior) and L2 (engine-boundary runtime semantics).

Task Objective:
Deliver the dual-layer lockstep oracle program: L1 runs Node/Bun/franken_node against shared fixture suites for externally visible behavior equivalence; L2 runs franken_engine boundary corpora against reference semantics for runtime-integrity guardrails. Both layers must have release-policy linkage so no release ships without green oracle status.

Detailed Acceptance Criteria:
1. L1 oracle: runs identical test scenarios across Node, Bun, and franken_node; captures and normalizes outputs; reports divergences with causal trace equivalence reports (9C.4).
2. L2 oracle: runs franken_engine boundary corpora against reference semantics; validates runtime invariants.
3. Release-policy linkage: release gates require both L1 and L2 green status.
4. Oracle delivery close condition: dual-layer oracle is only complete when L1 (10.2) + L2 (10.17) + release policy linkage (10.2) are all green.
5. Deterministic simulation and delta-debugging reductions to converge quickly on minimal divergence fixtures (9B.4).
6. Deterministic replay envelopes for each divergence decision (9C.4).
7. Streaming normalization and parallel fixture evaluation to reduce differential harness cost (9D.4).
8. Coverage spans all compatibility bands (core/high-value/edge/unsafe) with per-band pass/fail reporting.

Key Dependencies:
- L1 oracle canonical ownership: 10.2 (Compatibility Core).
- L2 oracle canonical ownership: 10.17 (Radical Expansion — engine-boundary N-version oracle).
- Release policy linkage: 10.2 release gates.
- Depends on 10.1 (Charter) for split-governance contract defining engine vs product boundaries.

Expected Artifacts:
- src/conformance/lockstep_oracle.rs — L1 oracle harness.
- Integration with franken_engine for L2 boundary oracle.
- Release gate configuration linking both layers.
- docs/specs/section_10_0/bd-uo4_contract.md
- artifacts/section_10_0/bd-uo4/verification_evidence.json

Testing and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests: output normalization, divergence detection, delta-debugging reduction, release gate evaluation.
- Integration tests: L1 three-runtime comparison on fixture suites, L2 engine boundary corpus validation.
- E2E tests: franken-node verify lockstep CLI producing divergence reports with per-band breakdown.
- Performance tests: parallel fixture evaluation throughput and memory profile.
- Structured logs: LOCKSTEP_L1_RUN, LOCKSTEP_L2_RUN, DIVERGENCE_DETECTED, DELTA_DEBUG_REDUCED, RELEASE_GATE_EVALUATED with trace IDs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. L1 (product boundary) oracle tracks: franken-node release versions, feature flags, API surface changes, deprecation schedule; emits compatibility matrix per release.
2. L2 (engine boundary) oracle tracks: underlying engine versions (V8/JavaScriptCore/SpiderMonkey), ABI changes, embedding API deltas; emits engine-compatibility matrix.
3. Release-policy linkage: each release decision references both L1 and L2 oracle outputs; policy gate blocks release if oracle detects unresolved breaking change.
4. Lockstep verification: oracle detects version skew between L1 product and L2 engine within 1 CI cycle; alerts with specific incompatibility details.
5. Oracle state is deterministic and reproducible: given same input versions, produces identical compatibility assessment.
6. Oracle outputs machine-readable compatibility report (JSON): { l1_version, l2_version, compatibility_status, breaking_changes[], policy_disposition, release_recommendation }.
7. Historical oracle decisions are append-only and auditable; queryable by version range and date range.
8. Integration with compatibility envelope (bd-1qp): oracle consumes envelope scores as input signal for release readiness.
9. Dual-layer coverage: oracle covers 100% of catalogued API surface in both L1 and L2 dimensions (Section 3 replay coverage target).
10. Verification evidence includes: oracle decision log for at least 3 simulated release scenarios, lockstep violation detection test, policy-gate enforcement test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:42.723311996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:10:02.236534474Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:10:02.236491374Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-0","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uo4","depends_on_id":"bd-al8i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T15:01:25.079028856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-uo4","depends_on_id":"bd-y4g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:10.224402686Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
